mechanism_narrative: A persistent federal funds rate below 1 percent compresses the risk-free discount rate and raises the present value of future corporate cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 1%, or the mean return in this regime is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 6.0 percent signals a tight labor market that drives wage income growth and sustained consumer spending, lifting corporate revenues and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 6.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the average return under the trigger does not exceed the unconditional average (Welch t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in industrial production and retail sales confirm a broad demand-supply expansion that reduces recession probability and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 105.2161 and RSAFS at 441363.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Joint upward moves in INDPRO and RSAFS fail to predict positive S&P 500 20-day returns with an accuracy above 55% over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power in a logistic regression (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1.0 percent, an accelerating consumer price index signals stagflation risk that cannot be countered by additional monetary easing, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, accelerating CPIAUCSL is followed by positive S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the mean return conditional on accelerating inflation is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 900 thousand units indicate a recovering residential construction sector that boosts employment in construction and durable goods consumption, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 900, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances, or the cumulative return 60 days after the trigger is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment combined with a falling unemployment rate signals labor market momentum that outpaces labor force growth, lifting household income and equity valuations via higher consumption and corporate earnings over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 141183.0 and UNRATE at 5.5% as of t-1mo
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to produce positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 55% of joint-signal occurrences over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term exhibits a Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A higher level of industrial production directly raises manufacturing profitability and capacity utilization, which translates into stronger aggregate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Higher INDPRO levels are followed by negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50% of monthly observations, or the rank correlation between INDPRO and 5-day forward returns is not positive and significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP exceeding 16.0 trillion chained 2017 dollars confirms that aggregate demand has recovered sufficiently to support trend corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 exceeds 16000, subsequent S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of quarterly observations, or the mean 60-day return conditional on this threshold is less than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).
